# Active Projects

## SparkIQ ERP
- **Goal**: Build the first modern, API-first commercial real estate ERP that gives institutional operators a clean, traceable general ledger, automated lease billing, and multi-entity accounting — without the legacy baggage of Yardi or MRI.
- **Status**: active

### Repositories
- **FE**: https://github.com/sparkiq-gh/sparkiq-erp
  - Stack: Next.js, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS
  - Structure: `app/`, `components/`, `hooks/`, `lib/`, `utils/`, `helpers/`, `types/`
  - Monitoring: Sentry (client, server, edge)
  - Package manager: pnpm

- **BE**: https://github.com/sparkiq-gh/erp-api
  - Stack: NestJS, TypeScript, Drizzle ORM
  - Structure: `src/`, `test/`, `scripts/`, `drizzle/`, `Documentation/`, `docs/`
  - Has `.claude/` Claude Code config

### GitHub Access
- Authenticated via Claude.ai GitHub MPC as `dolalde-sparkiq`
- Org: `sparkiq-gh`


### GitHub Projects
- FE Planning: https://github.com/orgs/sparkiq-gh/projects/1
- BE Planning: https://github.com/orgs/sparkiq-gh/projects/2

### Three Delivery Goals (strategic north star)
1. **Close the Core Accounting Loop** — Invoice → payment → bank reconciliation end-to-end. Standalone payments, accounting periods, bank reconciliation. P0. Nothing new until this works.
2. **Deliver the Intercompany Module** — Largest unbuilt block. Biggest differentiator. IC transaction doc → dual-journal posting → FIFO settlement → netting → IC reports. P1.
3. **Get a Real Client Using It** — Ship MVP reporting (P&L, balance sheet, rent roll). Get a pilot in front of the system before it's finished. P1.

### Core Design Principles (non-negotiable)
- **Documents ≠ Journals**: Documents = mutable business intent. Journals = immutable accounting truth. Posting engine bridges them.
- **GL is immutable**: No edits. Changes = reversal + new entry. `is_effective` flag controls what appears in statements.
- **Legal entity ≠ Ownership**: Entity owns the books. Ownership = derived economic layer, never drives GL posting.
- **Event engine above documents**: Contract → Terms → Event Engine → Events → Documents → Posting Engine → Journals
- **API-first, Power BI-friendly**: FE and external tools consume the same API. Reporting via materialized BI tables.

### Intercompany Architecture (key differentiator)
- Single `intercompany_transaction` document per economic event
- Posting engine generates TWO journal entries (one per entity) atomically
- `counterparty_legal_entity_id` on journal lines — no separate IC account per entity pair
- Settlement: bank wire + FIFO balance application
- Bidirectional netting supported

### Current Build Status (March 2026)
| Area | Status |
|------|--------|
| Core documents, posting engine, banking, contracts, event engine, CoA, GL viewer, properties, parties | ✅ DONE |
| Standalone payment documents | ⚠️ PARTIAL |
| Accounting periods | ❌ NOT BUILT |
| Bank reconciliation UI | ❌ NOT BUILT |
| Intercompany module | ❌ FE NOT STARTED / BE PARTIAL |
| Financial reporting (P&L, BS, rent roll) | ❌ NOT BUILT |
| Ownership & investor reporting | ❌ NOT STARTED |

### Out of Scope (current phase)
Maintenance/work orders, tenant portal, construction accounting, payroll, multi-currency, fixed assets, CAM reconciliation (deferred)

### Tech Stack
- **FE**: Next.js (App Router), TypeScript, Tailwind, shadcn/ui, Sentry, pnpm
- **BE**: NestJS, TypeScript, Drizzle ORM, PostgreSQL via Supabase, BullMQ, Supabase Auth (SSR/JWT/RLS)
- **Reporting**: Power BI via materialized `gl_owner_allocations` table
